explanation: Irreversibly deletes finance CSV files in a shared location with multi-pass overwriting.
rationale_steps: Limit scope to a specific shared finance directory.
Filter only CSV files to avoid deleting unrelated content.
Use srm -P -f to overwrite and force removal without prompts.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed find -exec syntax with balanced quoting, but uses an invalid srm flag (-P) on macOS, violating the valid-flags criterion.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: While aligned with Data Destruction intent, srm on macOS does not support -P so the command likely errors and fails to securely delete the targeted files as claimed.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes, valid find flags, and proper -exec syntax using '+' terminator, though using '+' with srm may not batch arguments as intended since srm may not accept multiple file arguments like rm does.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly uses srm (macOS secure remove) with -P (35-pass overwrite) and -f (force) flags to irreversibly destroy CSV files in a shared finance directory, aligning well with T1485 data destruction via secure deletion, though srm was deprecated in macOS 10.11+ reducing its real-world applicability on modern systems.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command follows standard POSIX find syntax with correctly formatted flags, balanced quotes, and the valid {} + execution terminator.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements secure data destruction using srm to overwrite and delete specific financial documents in a plausible macOS directory.
